title: Types of Bones (Long, Short, Flat, Irregular)

flashcards.definition: Longer than wide, with a shaft and two ends (e.g., femur, humerus).
Cube-shaped; found in wrist (carpals) and ankle (tarsals).
Thin, flattened, often curved; provides protection and broad surfaces (e.g., skull, sternum).
Complex, non-uniform shape (e.g., vertebrae, hip bones).
Small bone embedded within a tendon; the patella is the largest example.
